instruction:
m = 3, n = 2. Consider the following mapping $f = (f_{1}, f_{2}) : R^{5} \to R^{2}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
f_{1}(x_{1}, x_{2}, y_{1}, y_{2}, y_{3}) &= 2 e^{x_{1}} + x_{2} y_{1} - 4 y_{2} + 3 \\
f_{2}(x_{1}, x_{2}, y_{1}, y_{2}, y_{3}) &= x_{2} \cos x_{1} - 6 x_{1} + 2 y_{1} - y_{3}
\end{aligned}
$$
If $a = (0, 1)$, $b = (3, 2, 7)$ then $f(a, b) = 0$
Verify that $A_{x}$ is invertible. Therefore, by the implicit function theorem, a $C^{1}$ mapping defined in a neighborhood of (3, 2, 7) can be obtained. What is the $C^{1}$ mapping?

Options:
A. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{3} {5}}} & {{\frac{4} {8}}} & {{-\frac{1} {7}}} \\ {{\frac{5} {9}}} & {{\frac{-2} {3}}} & {{\frac{6} {1 5}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
B. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{2} {5}}} & {{\frac{-1} {6}}} & {{\frac{3} {5}}} \\ {{\frac{4} {8}}} & {{\frac{-3} {7}}} & {{\frac{1} {4}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
C. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{2} {9}}} & {{\frac{5} {7}}} & {{-\frac{1} {4}}} \\ {{\frac{3} {5}}} & {{\frac{-2} {6}}} & {{\frac{1} {8}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
D. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{3} {4}}} & {{\frac{1} {3}}} & {{-\frac{2} {5}}} \\ {{\frac{-1} {3}}} & {{\frac{4} {5}}} & {{\frac{1} {2}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
E. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{1} {4}}} & {{\frac{1} {5}}} & {{-\frac{3} {2 0}}} \\ {{\frac{-1} {2}}} & {{\frac{6} {5}}} & {{\frac{1} {1 0}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
F. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{1} {2}}} & {{\frac{2} {5}}} & {{-\frac{1} {1 0}}} \\ {{\frac{1} {4}}} & {{\frac{3} {4}}} & {{\frac{-1} {5}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
G. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{1} {3}}} & {{\frac{2} {7}}} & {{-\frac{4} {5}}} \\ {{\frac{0} {2}}} & {{\frac{5} {1 2}}} & {{\frac{3} {8}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
H. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{-2} {3}}} & {{\frac{3} {5}}} & {{-\frac{1} {6}}} \\ {{\frac{4} {6}}} & {{\frac{-5} {2}}} & {{\frac{3} {4}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
I. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{-1} {5}}} & {{\frac{2} {6}}} & {{\frac{1} {3}}} \\ {{\frac{2} {7}}} & {{\frac{-3} {1 0}}} & {{\frac{5} {9}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
J. $$
\left( \begin{array} {c c c} {{\frac{4} {1 0}}} & {{\frac{1} {4}}} & {{-\frac{3} {6}}} \\ {{\frac{5} {3}}} & {{-\frac{2} {7}}} & {{\frac{1} {5}}} \\ \end{array} \right)
$$
